# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/XcXKMiaRjaw

## Global backdrop
- **Gap-down opening expected.** Many Asian markets (China, Taiwan) closed for a local festival; GIFT Nifty signals weakness. It's Friday, last day of the week.
- **US markets closed positive** — Nasdaq strong on AI/chips: **Nvidia +3%, Intel +10%** (on news of a chip-manufacturing deal with Apple).
- **But Accenture took a beating** — cut FY revenue guidance (to 3–4% from 3–5%); West Asia war cost ~$100m of consulting revenue; raised acquisition spend to $9bn (from $5bn). Negative read-through for Indian IT: **Infosys ADR −10%, Globant −11%, EPAM −12%, Cognizant −10%, Capgemini −9%.**
- **Brent ~$79.55**, settled — won't rush to $70/$65 because strategic reserves (incl. US) must be replenished.
- US–Iran interim peace deal effective from today; key is Strait of Hormuz goods flow returning to pre-war levels. Kutumba Rao notes the "Treaty of Versailles 2.0" parallel (one-sided deals can sow future conflict).

## Indian markets
- **5th straight gaining session** — Nifty +82 yesterday; ~1,000-point rally from 23,000 to above 24,000. Profit-booking session likely (IT drag + crude disappointment).
- **FII selling has eased** — only ₹1,000 cr sold yesterday (vs ₹4,000–5,000 cr earlier); **DIIs bought ₹3,517 cr.** Mid-cap index at all-time high, small-cap near it.
- **Reliance 49th AGM today** — market awaits Jio Platforms listing/IPO timeline commentary.
- **Direct tax collections +15%** YoY — a positive for the government despite headwinds.
- **NSE IPO sellers/beneficiaries:** SBI (could net ~₹5,000 cr "jackpot"), Morgan Stanley/Mauritius, CPPIB, Bank of Baroda, Stock Holding Corp, GIC, New India Assurance (~₹2,157 cr — was a top Nifty-500 gainer). **LIC (10.72%, ~₹5,400 cr) not selling**; nor are Radhakishan Damani (~₹8,032 cr) or Sunil Kant Munjal (~₹2,000 cr).
- Curiosity: a shareholder (Kashmiri Lal Rana) had 5,000 NSE shares wrongly credited (Dec 2023), refuses to return them; sold 3,685 — NSE now in a legal fight to recover ₹1.43 cr.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Kranthi & Vasanth** — discussed **Olectra Greentech** (capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement tailwind → potential 2–3-bagger; "picked it first" from ~90 to 2,200); plus some viewer Q&A (Divi's Lab, NHPC/NCC/IRFC, HDFC, SEMA India).
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — Nifty bullish toward **24,700** (support 23,800); **Bank Nifty toward 60,000 zone** (RSI 70.97, first >70 since Nov; resistance only at 60,400–500). Buy-on-dips; go long intraday only above 24,120–130.
- Market opened weak — **Nifty −200, IT index −6%**, breadth negative; HFCL hit 5% upper circuit, Natco Pharma +2.5%.

kutumba.note: NSE stake ~Rs 5,400 cr (=10% of LIC mkt cap); signs of +10% once NSE issue factors into NAV.
kutumba.detail: Speculative-longs / watch list. NSE stake value alone is ~Rs 5,400 cr, which is 10% of LIC's market cap; once the NSE issue happens that gets factored into NAV, so LIC has signs of rising 10%. Stock may go up to 460 or 470 in the near-to-medium term; don't ignore it.

kutumba.note: Multibagger / 2-3 bagger potential; capacity expansion, electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, old-bus replacement.
kutumba.detail: Sees more than 15-20%/yr - multibagger returns; from here a likely 2-3 bagger. Capacity expansion underway, entering electric trucks, BYD battery tie-up, and India's hard line on China plus old-bus/vehicle replacement (Delhi, Mumbai) are tailwinds. Picked it first from ~90.

kutumba.note: Not a great/wealth-creating stock; rises seasonally then falls. Hold.
kutumba.detail: I'd say hold; recently activity in the stock has increased. But these aren't great stocks to buy and not wealth-creating - they rise seasonally when the market is good and then fall.

kutumba.note: Only for positional trading; IOC/BPCL/HPCL are better bets for the future.
kutumba.detail: Only for positional trading; the stock moves very swiftly (1,000+ to ~750 then back above 1,100). Parent-controlled with fixed margins so the valuation holds, but if you ask me IOC, BPCL or HPCL would be better bets in the future.

kutumba.note: Don't buy large quantity now; range-bound, underperforming. Wait for June-end results.
kutumba.detail: In such large quantity, not immediately. The company is stabilising slowly but not showing expected performance, so the stock is range-bound; it has held above 200 for many days. I would wait for the June-end result before deciding.

kutumba.note: On-and-off performer; dividend play. Don't enter with great hopes.
kutumba.detail: ITC and IOC perform on-and-off and can lie flat for a couple of years. In IOC there's a dividend play, especially as crude prices keep moving back and forth; my advice is don't step in with great hopes on these.

kutumba.note: Speculative but fundamentally fine; Rs 10 paid-up, operator-driven. Hold; buying fresh is a gamble.
kutumba.detail: Fundamentally looks fine; put it as a slightly speculative bet. Rode the recent CCL rally to 180 then corrected to ~150; being a Rs 10 paid-up stock some activity continues, so hold for some time. Buying fresh is at the operator's mercy - it's in his fist.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 18 June 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5pa0Yll0Hm4

## Global backdrop
- Modi and Trump met in France after a 16-month gap. Little of substance, but Trump was warm on India ("great friend," called Modi "an angel" and "a killer" negotiator). An Iran–US ceasefire MoU was signed; the formal agreement is due "tomorrow." India–US relations, frosty three months ago, look back on track.
- **FOMC:** Kevin Warsh chaired his first meeting and was hawkish — rates held, but a hint of **one rate hike in 2026** (9 of 18 officials expect it; markets eye ~40–50 bps, possibly October). 10-yr yield ~4.5%, Dollar Index back above 100.
- **US equities fell**, led by tech/AI (Nvidia, hyperscalers); Nasdaq down. Focus shifting to the ~$700bn AI capex cycle (Microsoft, Alphabet, Meta, Oracle) and the debt-vs-equity question behind it. Brent ~$78.

## Indian markets
- 4th straight gaining session: Nifty +~100, Sensex +350, **Bank Nifty the standout (+~1.3%)**. Nifty poised near a breakout around 24,088 (next resistance ~24,247 / 100-DMA ~24,232).
- **Heavy IPO/primary-market pipeline:** NSE IPO filed (~₹30,000 cr, likely the largest ever), Jio Platforms (~₹38,000 cr) papers expected this week, plus ~$26bn of lock-in shares releasing over coming weeks. Reliance AGM tomorrow.
- India became the **6th-largest market cap (~$5T)**; South Korea edged ahead, Taiwan still above (~$5.55T).
- Theme: large-caps are institution-driven (valuation-sensitive); mid/small-caps are retail-driven (story-driven, valuation-blind) — watch the hype.

## Indian markets — previous session and today
- Prior session: Nifty -243 pts (closed ~23,123, slightly below the April 8 / 23,150 support); Sensex -719 pts (~0.9–1% loss). India relatively outperformed Korea/Taiwan.
- FIIs sold ~Rs 5,556 cr; DIIs bought ~Rs 5,165 cr (net mild selling). FII index shorts built up from 1.53 lakh contracts (May 26) to 2.78 lakh — ~92% shorts vs 8% longs.

- MSCI EM top-10: For the first time in 26 years no Indian company is in the MSCI EM top-10. HDFC Bank now 11th, Reliance 12th, as Samsung (+146% YTD) and SK Hynix (+193% YTD) weightings surged.

Per analyst Kutumba Rao, a ~Rs 28,000 cr cluster project; Korean and possibly Russian firms may also participate.

- Telecom firm on the levy ruling (Vodafone Idea, IRB Infra, Tata Communications, IDFC First Bank, Bharti Hexacom among mid-cap gainers).
- Pharma and infrastructure/engineering showed resilience in the prior session.

OI: highest call writing at 23,250, highest put writing at 23,000 — Nifty to oscillate in that zone; Bank Nifty in a 300–400 pt range (54,200–54,500). > Note: Analyst **Kutumba Rao was ABSENT** in this episode. Analyst commentary was provided by **Kranti**; technicals by **Ramakrishna**.

**Market backdrop.**
- Last trading day of May; weak open expected (Gift Nifty ~−100 pts after being −300 intraday yesterday on renewed Iran–US strikes — Iran fired a ballistic missile at Kuwait, intercepted). But global cues were positive: Nasdaq at a record high (AI rally), Korea/Taiwan led by tech (TSMC ~45% of Taiwan's cap), crude low at ~$93, dollar index ~99.
- **AI momentum:** Dell raised AI-server revenue guidance from $10bn to $60bn (6x); Anthropic raised funds at ~$965bn valuation (run-rate ~$47bn) vs OpenAI ~$860bn; Korean 12-layer HBM4 sold out. Wipro ADR +18% on a ServiceNow agentic-AI tie-up. India = large AI *user*, not maker; ~5 GW of data-centre capacity expected in 4-5 years → plays in servers, network tech, electrical equipment, cables, gensets.
- **Domestic:** FII selling easing (Mar 1.18 lakh cr → Apr 61k cr → May ~34k cr; Wed only ~1k cr). Record FY26 listed-bank profits (~4.1 lakh cr). Strong results season (Business Standard Q4 sectoral scorecard). Financial Express: steps to boost capital inflows likely within a week; possible review of 12.5% LTCG for foreign investors. Defence action shifting from PSU (HAL, Cochin Shipyard, BDL) to private names (Data Patterns, Astra Micro, Apollo Micro). Coal India OFS institutional portion ~8x subscribed (floor 412, cut-off ~447).
- **Caption Services:** FY revenue +21% (831 cr), EBITDA +43%, PAT +57% (28.13 cr); entering home services (Hyderabad launch June 19) — TAM ~51-52k cr but initially loss-making; 12-month target 480-500 (bull), 400-450 (base).

**Kranti's view (in lieu of Kutumba Rao):** There IS a bull market in India — but in breadth (bottom of the pyramid, mid/small-caps up ~25-30% off lows), not the top 50/100. A stock-picker's market; run profits, stay vigilant (not a "buy-and-sleep" market). Crude is "the joker in the pack" — decisive cool-off (toward $80) would be a big relief; above $100 turns bearish. If results stay positive next quarter, India could turn attractive to FIIs again.

**Technical (Ramakrishna):** Market stuck in a symmetrical triangle; 24,100 the key upside trigger (then 24,600), downside support 23,706 (OI suggests 23,900). Momentum indicators (RSI/MACD/histogram) positive; holding 20-day EMA; VIX cooled ~7% (~14.98), PCR ~1.02; breadth strong; net strong-hand flows positive (~+2,800). Breakout either side likely by June 2.

- It's Friday (weekend factor). US markets closed positive last night; Nikkei, Kospi and Taiwan trading up.

- **Gap-up open expected** (GIFT Nifty +120–130). US closed strong, Asia positive. Main driver: **crude fell ~5% overnight to ~$105–106 (Brent $105.96)** — an "oil-relief rally." Trump says Iran peace talks are in their "final stage" (credibility doubtful).

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 19 May 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/SOI0mRnZij8

## Global backdrop
- **Iran:** Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE asked the US to hold off; Trump halted planned strikes "from today" — another back-and-forth. Iran put an updated peace proposal to the US and is negotiating tough; it set up a "Persian Gulf State Authority" to keep control of the Strait of Hormuz.
- **US markets** closed mixed — memory-chip stocks sold off, Dow positive. Asian markets mixed. **Crude below $110** (had hit ~$112 on the halt-strikes news); India raised diesel +91 paise, petrol +87 paise.
- US gave a **third extension of the Russian-oil waiver**; India firmly reaffirmed it will keep buying Russian oil for energy security.
- Conditions remain unstable; markets very volatile.

## Indian markets
- Sharp intraday recovery: **Nifty +~300, Sensex +~1,000**, closed green, **led by IT** (Coforge, Persistent, then Tech Mahindra, Infosys, Wipro) plus Bharti Airtel and the Bajaj twins.
- FIIs net +Rs 2,682 cr, DIIs +Rs 2,814 cr — but largely block deals (GQG took 1.5 cr JSW Steel shares at Rs 1,260 ≈ Rs 1,900 cr; SBI MF 1 cr shares at 1,260), so underlying buying was thin.
- **Small-caps the worry:** advance/decline 785 vs 2,355; small-cap index −1.26% (−1.95% MTD) after a 30–50% run over ~45 days — profit-booking.
- **Adani group in the limelight:** US court threw out the fraud allegations (clean chit); Adani to pay ~$2.5m penalty for Iranian LPG shipments — big relief; group stocks opened up.
- Today is **Nifty weekly options expiry / settlement day** → volatility expected. Market opened positive (Nifty +70, Sensex +~300, Bank Nifty +110).
- **Result season:** >50–55% of results comfortable. Good: GE Vernova, Samhi, JK Paper, Puravankara (+14%, revenue-recognition change), Indian Oil, Visaka, Garuda Construction, Apollo Micro (+4%, but watch negative operating cash flow), Astral, Sheshasayee Tech. Poor: Afcons Infra (−5–6%). Due today: BEL, BPCL, Mankind Pharma, Zydus Life, PI Industries.
- Notable macro: a year ago 3 Indian firms were in the world top-100 by market cap (RIL 57, HDFC 73, TCS 84); now none (RIL 106, HDFC 190, TCS 314). Many IT stocks near 2008 sub-prime-crisis levels.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets + viewer calls) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file. Headline view: stay invested, be stock-specific, avoid cats-and-dogs, keep exiting weaker stocks; banks/NBFCs/autos attractive for a 2–3 year view.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — markets bouncing back inside the Bollinger Bands; Nifty reversed at the 50% retracement near 23,300, target 24,000 (20-DMA); 23,400 immediate support; expects to stay above 24,000 into monthly settlement. RSI ~45 (needs to cross 50). VIX +4.47% to ~19.63; PCR 1.24. Triple-whammy macro risk (bond yields up, crude up, rupee weak at 96.36) → a Nifty 23,000 retest is highest-probability if it persists 2–3 weeks. Suggested index longs with stop near 23,650.
- Side note: an extended on-air discussion about IT firms hiring "forward-deployed engineers" (Palantir/Coforge) and the anchors/analysts learning to use AI tools (Claude, Anthropic Academy courses, Gemini, Perplexity).

kutumba.note: Buy immediately, accumulate slowly; serious 2-year wait, don't panic on interim corrections.
kutumba.detail: Buy immediately / accumulate slowly from here; needs a serious 2-year view, don't panic when corrections come in between.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 21 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/3MGecfbr-R8

## Global backdrop
- **US-Iran talks** scheduled for "tomorrow"; JD Vance left for Pakistan and an Iran delegation is expected (per WSJ). The ceasefire deadline expires around Thursday morning IST — markets watching whether a deal is struck or the ceasefire is extended.
- US markets closed in the red, but Dow/Nasdaq futures and GIFT Nifty point to a positive open (~60-70 pts).
- **Crude:** Brent ~$95, Nymex ~$88-89; physical market eased from $140-145 toward ~$120 as nations cut consumption ("demand destruction") and focus on energy security.
- **Tech:** Apple — John Ternus becomes CEO, Tim Cook (15 yrs, ~20x market-cap growth) moves to executive chairman. Amazon to invest $5bn in Anthropic (maker of Claude AI); Anthropic to use $100bn of AWS in return.

## Indian markets
- Yesterday volatile, closed with small gains (Nifty +11-12 pts after a negative open and intraday +120-130). **Today is the Nifty 50 expiry.**
- RBI eased the April-1 derivatives restrictions → banks can offer offshore derivative contracts again; **rupee saw its steepest one-day fall** and weakness may resume.
- Bank Nifty faced a **200-DMA rejection**; heavy put writing means Nifty faces strong resistance higher up.
- **FIIs net buyers (~Rs 1,000 cr); DIIs net sellers (~Rs 3,000 cr)** — but a low-volume day.
- **Vedanta** to split into five companies (Oil & Gas, Aluminium, etc.); record date May 1.
- Results: PNB Housing, Bank of Maharashtra, Navkar Corp, E2E Networks, Aditya Birla Money all reported good numbers; PN Gadgil positive Akshaya Tritiya update. HCL Tech, Persistent, 360 One reporting today.
- Open: Nifty +39, Sensex +208, Bank Nifty +315 (later Nifty +118, Sensex +400); Nifty IT down 4%, mid/small-caps strong.

## Analysts featured
- **Kutumba Rao** (fundamental/markets) — see the separate Kutumba Rao file.
- **Kranti** (new co-analyst, joined late) — market in a consolidation phase around 24,000; no disappointments in results so far (IT subdued); momentum to pick up; crude ~$85 favourable.
- **Ramakrishna** (technicals) — higher-high/higher-low structure intact; 24,415 gap filled, next gap ~25,100; chance to cross 50-DMA. Brent below $100 digested; above $100 = danger bells. Bank Nifty trying to fill the March-6 gap (57,000 put). Buy-on-dips; Nifty supports 24,271 / 24,100 / 23,900; expiry range bounded by 24,500 call / 24,300 put.

## Theme
Energy/power a multi-year theme (Hitachi Energy, Siemens Energy, Quality Power, Atlanta Electricals, TD Power); identify early. Mid/small-cap season — "pick your right stock and invest." Avoid getting tempted into illiquid real-estate bets.

kutumba.note: Good bet on every decline; confidently targets servicing 25% of GDP by 2030.
kutumba.detail: Even being a PSU bank, SBI confidently reports it will service 25% of GDP by 2030 — shows how aggressively it thinks. A good bet on every decline; no need to think too much.

kutumba.note: Value-unlocking signs (HDFC Sec, AMC, Life); at not even 11-12 PE, will attract buyers.
kutumba.detail: Largest private bank, competitively placed; value-unlocking signs given subsidiaries (HDFC Securities, HDFC AMC) and already-divested HDFC Life. Including investment value it's available at not even 11-12 PE; will attract buyers at some point.

kutumba.note: Up ~150% from earlier call; high-PE (~86-87) but performing; buy near 900 on any post-result dip.
kutumba.detail: At 52-week high, ~150% return from his earlier call; now high-PE (~86-87) but performing in line with results (profit Rs 65 cr Sep to ~Rs 94 cr Dec; Q4 to come well; new France JV). Forms higher tops/bottoms — any post-result correction close to 900 is a good entry.

# Summary — Business Breakfast, 7 April 2026 (TV5 Money)
# https://youtu.be/5XNyutWROjg

## Global backdrop (war footing)
- The Iran–US (West Asia) war is in its **sixth week** (~37–38 days). Trump set yet another deadline ("8 pm Eastern tonight") threatening to bomb Iran's power plants and bridges. Anchor treats it as empty bluster — Trump declared an unsolicited ceasefire ~12–15 days ago and may do so again; "a well in front, a ditch behind" — either he escalates (crude spikes) or backs down (looks weak).
- Why a quick end matters: Iran has been hitting Gulf energy infrastructure **and AI/web infrastructure** — already destroyed "Amazon servers," now eyeing the **$500bn Stargate AI project in the UAE**; the AI revolution could take a blow. Iran's ultimate weapon is **closing the Strait of Hormuz**.
- FM **Nirmala Sitharaman**: the war has "evolved into a systemic tremor," but India has "enough fiscal space to support war-hit sectors."

## Indian markets
- Tuesday = **Nifty options expiry**. Yesterday the Nifty bounced ~255 pts (led by HDFC Bank, Axis, ICICI, L&T) despite **Reliance dragging it down ~90 pts**; closed just below 23,000.
- **FIIs relentless sellers:** Rs 8,167 cr yesterday; **Rs 1.17 lakh cr in March** (~Rs 60,000 cr of it financials), continuing into April. DIIs bought only Rs 889 cr. Domestic funds/MFs are positioned for a relief rally.
- Recent higher highs: 22,679 (Apr 1) → 22,713 (Apr 2) → 22,968 (yesterday).
- Markets opened **very weak today**: Nifty ~-220 (22,750), Sensex ~-940; only Wipro & Hindalco green in the index. VIX still elevated >25; crude not cooling — **NYMEX (~113-115) now trades above Brent (~111)**, a war-driven reversal.
- Macro red flags: Moody's cut GDP to **6%**; govt planning a **Rs 2.5 lakh cr credit-guarantee (stimulus-like) scheme** for MSMEs; fertilizer shortage / rabi-crop uncertainty; induction-stove subsidies hint at a worsening **LPG crisis**; election-driven **labour crunch** (Assam, West Bengal, Kerala, Tamil Nadu) stoking inflation. **RBI policy meeting tomorrow.**
- Q4 business updates strong in jewellery (Titan, Kalyan, PC Jeweller) and Jubilant FoodWorks. Notable single-day pops: Senco Gold +10-12%, Lux Industries +20%, Venky's +12%, Banka BioLoo 10% UC, Vishal Mega Mart +10%.
